notes: v1.14.4 — first-run setup hardening (storage-full recovery). firstRun_confirm() previously dead-ended on a failed siteProfile_save (storage full): only a transient toast, no recovery, operator stuck on the setup overlay. Added a persistent inline error in the setup form (#fr-error) that, on save failure, tells the operator the device storage is full and to export a backup + clear old deployments then re-tap CONFIRM & ENTER; cleared at the start of each attempt. Success path and all other flows unchanged. CONTEXT: the QA-flagged 'setup wedge after Master load' was investigated and found to be a HEADLESS TEST-HARNESS ARTIFACT, not a code bug — firstRun_confirm() is Master-independent and a tiny profile save can't fail with normal storage; this hardening addresses the genuine (rare) storage-full edge it surfaced. Verified: node --check 3/3 inline scripts, CSS braces balanced, CRLF preserved (0 lone LF), four stamps lockstep v1.14.4, sw.js cache-bumped. NOT runtime-tested (a real localStorage quota failure couldn't be triggered in the sandbox). WARNING: repo-only change — the external/web-Claude build base lacks this AND the v1.13.8 #boot full-bleed fix; both will be dropped on the next handoff unless baked into that base.
